behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because the failure is at a module handoff (diff parser -> review-input builder), not just isolated logic. Downgrade check: Unit is possible only if this were one pure function, but the behavior is explicitly about produced review input across changed files. Upgrade check: no behavior-stated need for real OS/network/subprocess or real model-quality dependence, so System/Agentic are rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixture PR diff with mixed changed/unchanged regions, run the real diff-loading + input-construction modules together, and assert the payload sent to the reviewer contains only changed hunks/lines.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: System, because correctness depends on real GitHub diff-position semantics over real HTTP. Downgrade check: Integration with mocked API can miss true position-mapping failures. Upgrade check: no behavior text requires model-output quality or multiple agentic steps, so Agentic/Workflow are rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments via the automation and verify each appears on the intended file/hunk/line in the PR diff view (via API readback and/or UI check).

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because the behavior depends on real model judgment ('identifies a real defect'). Downgrade check: Integration with stubs can always pass and hide detection failure. Upgrade check: behavior asks for defect identification, not a multi-step agent pipeline requirement, so Workflow is rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Run the reviewer with a real model on a seeded-bug PR and assert at least one finding correctly matches the known defect location/category.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is cross-module consistency (inline-comment publisher <-> summary composer). Downgrade check: Unit is too low if summary and inline outputs are produced by separate modules/state. Upgrade check: behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess or real model-quality validation; it is a correspondence invariant, so System/Agentic/Workflow are rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Run review orchestration with a fake GitHub client that records posted inline and top-level comments, then assert each summarized finding maps to an actually posted inline comment identifier/location.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Workflow, because this is explicitly the full agent pipeline on a real PR, combining agentic review behavior with multiple downstream actions in sequence. Downgrade check: System alone would validate API plumbing but can miss pipeline-level agent step interactions; Agentic alone would miss end-to-end action sequencing. Upgrade check: no higher level exists.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the automation end-to-end on a real test PR: fetch diff, generate findings, post inline comments, post summary; verify completion status and that expected artifacts exist without human edits/retries.
